message:
fix(sync): validate mode args before touching DB; drop stale reopen_at

Three related fixes in the `br sync` command and the SQLite storage
layer that together make `--rebuild` behave correctly when combined
with `--status` or invalid mode flags, and make import counts
truthful.

1. Hoist mode-flag validation above every side-effect

   The mutual-exclusion checks for `--flush-only` / `--import-only` /
   `--merge` and the "`--rebuild` only with import" check used to run
   *after* `open_storage_with_cli` and, more importantly, after the
   pre-import `recover_database_from_jsonl` block. That meant
   `br sync --flush-only --rebuild` (and similar misuse) returned the
   correct validation error — but only after the DB family had already
   been backed up and rebuilt. The validation now runs at the very top
   of `execute`, before `discover_beads_dir_with_cli` / `open_storage`
   / the rebuild shortcut, so a rejected invocation leaves the storage
   completely untouched.

2. Keep `sync --status` read-only even with `--rebuild`

   The pre-import `--rebuild` shortcut fired whenever the flag was set,
   including when `--status` was also passed. That made the
   documented-read-only status query silently trigger a full DB
   rebuild. The shortcut now additionally requires `!args.status`, so
   `br sync --status --rebuild` stays a pure read.

3. Report the post-rebuild row count, not the JSONL line count

   When the open-time rebuild short-circuits `execute_import`, the
   `ImportResultOutput.created` field was populated from
   `count_issues_in_jsonl(jsonl_path)`. That diverges from reality
   whenever the JSONL contains tombstones or otherwise-skipped rows,
   and it forces a second multi-MB parse of the JSONL right after the
   rebuild already consumed it. Switch to `storage.count_all_issues()`
   so the reported count reflects what is actually in the DB, and drop
   the redundant JSONL scan.

Storage cleanup
---------------

Remove `SqliteStorage::reopen_at` and its now-unused `db_lock_timeout`
threading through `execute_import`. The reopen dance was only needed by
the old in-place `reset_data_tables` + `import_from_jsonl` rebuild path;
that path was retired in favour of `recover_database_from_jsonl` (which
opens a fresh connection on its own), so the helper has no remaining
callers. Dropping it also removes the last place where sync.rs had to
carry a lock-timeout value purely for a rebuild reopen.

New regression test
-------------------

`tests/e2e_raw_fsqlite_rebuilt_lookup.rs` opens the raw fsqlite
connection after a `br init` + 20 `br create` + post-rebuild flow and
asserts that a keyed lookup (`WHERE id = ?`) returns the same rows as
a full scan, plus that `query_row_with_params` finds the same row.
This is the exact invariant that the full-scan recovery fallback
(#256) was supposed to guarantee — the new test catches regressions
in that path without depending on the CLI output format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,167 @@
+mod common;
+
+use common::cli::{BrWorkspace, run_br};
+use fsqlite::Connection;
+use fsqlite_types::SqliteValue;
+use serde_json::Value;
+
+fn extract_json(run_stdout: &str) -> Value {
+    let payload = common::cli::extract_json_payload(run_stdout);
+    serde_json::from_str(&payload).expect("valid cli json payload")
+}
+
+fn scan_issue_ids(conn: &Connection) -> Vec<String> {
+    conn.query("SELECT id FROM issues ORDER BY rowid")
+        .unwrap()
+        .into_iter()
+        .filter_map(|row| {
+            row.values()
+                .first()
+                .and_then(SqliteValue::as_text)
+                .map(ToOwned::to_owned)
+        })
+        .collect()
+}
+
+fn keyed_issue_ids(conn: &Connection, id: &str) -> Vec<String> {
+    conn.query_with_params(
+        "SELECT id FROM issues WHERE id = ?",
+        &[SqliteValue::from(id)],
+    )
+    .unwrap()
+    .into_iter()
+    .filter_map(|row| {
+        row.values()
+            .first()
+            .and_then(SqliteValue::as_text)
+            .map(ToOwned::to_owned)
+    })
+    .collect()
+}
+
+fn keyed_issue_row(conn: &Connection, id: &str) -> Option<String> {
+    conn.query_row_with_params(
+        "SELECT id FROM issues WHERE id = ?",
+        &[SqliteValue::from(id)],
+    )
+    .ok()
+    .and_then(|row| {
+        row.values()
+            .first()
+            .and_then(SqliteValue::as_text)
+            .map(ToOwned::to_owned)
+    })
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn e2e_raw_fsqlite_keyed_lookup_matches_full_scan_after_alt_rebuild() {
+    const LOOP_COUNT: usize = 20;
+
+    let _log = common::test_log("e2e_raw_fsqlite_keyed_lookup_matches_full_scan_after_alt_rebuild");
+    let workspace = BrWorkspace::new();
+
+    let init = run_br(&workspace, ["init", "--prefix", "raw"], "init_raw_workspace");
+    assert!(init.status.success(), "init failed: {}", init.stderr);
+
+    for title in ["seed A", "seed B"] {
+        let create = run_br(
+            &workspace,
+            [
+                "create",
+                "--title",
+                title,
+                "--type",
+                "task",
+                "--priority",
+                "2",
+                "--json",
+            ],
+            &format!("create_{title}"),
+        );
+        assert!(
+            create.status.success(),
+            "seed create failed for {title}: stdout={} stderr={}",
+            create.stdout,
+            create.stderr
+        );
+    }
+
+    let flush = run_br(&workspace, ["sync", "--flush-only"], "flush_before_alt_rebuild");
+    assert!(flush.status.success(), "flush failed: {}", flush.stderr);
+
+    let alt_db = workspace.root.join(".beads").join("beads.raw-rebuilt.db");
+    let rebuild = run_br(
+        &workspace,
+        [
+            "--db",
+            alt_db.to_str().expect("alt db path"),
+            "sync",
+            "--import-only",
+            "--rebuild",
+            "--json",
+            "--no-auto-import",
+            "--no-auto-flush",
+        ],
+        "rebuild_alt_db",
+    );
+    assert!(
+        rebuild.status.success(),
+        "alt rebuild failed: stdout={} stderr={}",
+        rebuild.stdout,
+        rebuild.stderr
+    );
+
+    for i in 0..LOOP_COUNT {
+        let title = format!("raw lookup loop {i}");
+        let create = run_br(
+            &workspace,
+            [
+                "--db",
+                alt_db.to_str().expect("alt db path"),
+                "create",
+                "--title",
+                &title,
+                "--type",
+                "task",
+                "--priority",
+                "2",
+                "--json",
+            ],
+            &format!("loop_create_{i}"),
+        );
+        assert!(
+            create.status.success(),
+            "create failed on loop {i}: stdout={} stderr={}",
+            create.stdout,
+            create.stderr
+        );
+        let create_json = extract_json(&create.stdout);
+        let fresh_id = create_json["id"]
+            .as_str()
+            .expect("fresh id")
+            .to_string();
+
+        let conn = Connection::open(alt_db.to_string_lossy().into_owned()).unwrap();
+
+        let scanned = scan_issue_ids(&conn);
+        assert!(
+            scanned.iter().any(|existing| existing == &fresh_id),
+            "full scan could not find freshly created id {fresh_id} on loop {i}"
+        );
+
+        let keyed = keyed_issue_ids(&conn, &fresh_id);
+        assert_eq!(
+            keyed,
+            vec![fresh_id.clone()],
+            "raw fsqlite keyed query_with_params diverged from full scan for {fresh_id} on loop {i}; scanned_tail={:?}",
+            &scanned[scanned.len().saturating_sub(10)..]
+        );
+
+        let keyed_row = keyed_issue_row(&conn, &fresh_id);
+        assert_eq!(
+            keyed_row.as_deref(),
+            Some(fresh_id.as_str()),
+            "raw fsqlite query_row_with_params diverged for {fresh_id} on loop {i}; keyed={keyed:?}"
+        );
+    }
+}
